name: reactome
description: Reactome — pathway analysis, gene list enrichment, identifier mapping, reaction participants, pathway hierarchy, diagram export, and full-text search. Backed by the public Reactome Analysis + Content Services (https://reactome.org/ContentService/ + https://reactome.org/AnalysisService/). Vendored from the google-deepmind/science-skills bundle.
author: zorai

db-name.description: Get the Reactome database name (sanity check; confirms the API is reachable).

python.command: python scripts/reactome_analysis.py db-name --output "${RE_OUTPUT:?set RE_OUTPUT (output file path)}"

db-version.description: Get the Reactome database version (useful for citing in publications).

analyze.description: Submit a gene list for pathway enrichment analysis. Returns a token you can use with the other token-* commands. The core operation — most workflows start here.

token-result.description: Retrieve the full analysis result by token (the path the original analysis used; default = species' main pathway hierarchy).

python.command: python scripts/reactome_analysis.py token-result --token "${RE_TOKEN:?set RE_TOKEN (token from /analyze)}" --output "${RE_OUTPUT:?set RE_OUTPUT}" ${RE_PATHWAY:+--pathway $RE_PATHWAY}

token-found-all.description: List all identifiers in the analysis result that mapped to Reactome (with the pathways they hit).

token-not-found.description: List identifiers in the analysis result that did NOT map to any Reactome pathway (useful for QC of an input gene list).

token-filter-species.description: Re-filter an analysis result by species (e.g. keep only Homo sapiens results).

python.command: python scripts/reactome_analysis.py token-filter-species --token "${RE_TOKEN:?set RE_TOKEN}" --species-filter "${RE_SPECIES:?set RE_SPECIES (NCBI taxon ID, e.g. 9606 for Homo sapiens)}" --output "${RE_OUTPUT:?set RE_OUTPUT}"

identifier.description: Analyze a single identifier (e.g. 'TP53') and return its pathway hits directly, without the two-step token dance.

python.command: python scripts/reactome_analysis.py identifier --id "${RE_ID:?set RE_ID (single gene/protein ID)}" --output "${RE_OUTPUT:?set RE_OUTPUT}" ${RE_RESOURCE:+--resource $RE_RESOURCE}

search.description: Full-text search across the Reactome knowledgebase (pathways, reactions, proteins).

python.command: python scripts/reactome_analysis.py search --query "${RE_QUERY:?set RE_QUERY (search string)}" --output "${RE_OUTPUT:?set RE_OUTPUT}"

top-pathways.description: List the top-level Reactome pathways (the 20-ish super-pathways like 'Signal Transduction', 'Disease').

run.description: Generic catch-all: forward an arbitrary reactome_analysis.py subcommand. Use for the 44+ specialized endpoints not exposed as named commands (analyze-projection, analyze-form, token-filter-pathways, token-found-entities, token-found-interactors, token-page, token-binned, download-result, download-pathways, mapping, mapping-projection, import-json, report, species-comparison, participants, participating-entities, component-of, event-ancestors, contained-events, low-pathways, xref-mapping, xref-mapping-batch, diagram, reaction-diagram, etc.).
